name: Vincent Lopez
abstract: Vincent Lopez (December 30, 1895 – September 20, 1975) was an American bandleader, actor, and pianist.

about_quotes.speaker: William T. (Bill) Challis
about_quotes.evidence: who walks in but Vincent Lopez... and he comes in with the manager or some such thing and listens to the band, likes the band and he says, 'They're going to open up a hotel here. Hotel Syracuse. How would you like the job?'

about_quotes.speaker: Sammy Price
about_quotes.evidence: being in the same town with Duke Ellington, Count Basie, Liberate or whoever in the hell was great...and Vincent Lopez and going down to a company...the same company that Guy Lombardo was

about_quotes.speaker: Zilner T. Randolph
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] I remember, when I was a kid, seeing a movie short of -- first time I had ever seen Betty Hutton. She was still with Vincent Lopez' band. [INTERVIEWEE] Yes.

about_quotes.speaker: Henry A. "Hank" Kmen
about_quotes.evidence: Vincent Lopez. Uh, he's still going, you know. He ought to write a book, that guy, because he's been around an awful lot of music for an awful long time.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: men like Ted Lewis, Ross Garner, Lopez, Vincent Lopez, former jazz bands, who copied after the style of the original Dixieland jazz bands recording

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: Vincent Lopez played at one of the hotels. It was the funniest thing to go in and see Vincent Lopez as a piano player with a piano accompanist.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
